Title: xCatalog - Search Products Query
Preconditions: BROWSER-ONLY: All tests MUST execute in GraphiQL UI via browser (playwright-edge). Do NOT use curl/API calls. Follow graphiql-interaction.md for CodeMirror interaction. GraphiQL UI accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; products in catalog
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" query: "laptop") { totalCount items { id name code imgSrc } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response from response panel
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] data.products object present in response
[DATA] totalCount > 0 for valid query term
[DATA] items[] non-empty; each item contains id, name, code
[DATA] imgSrc is a valid URL or null
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → same results returned
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; totalCount = 0 for known query term; missing id/name/code on items; GraphiQL shows error banner

Title: xCatalog - Get Product Detail Query
Preconditions: Product ID known from GQL-001; GraphiQL UI loaded
Test_Data: storeId={{STORE_ID}}; productId from GQL-001 result; currencyCode="USD"
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { product(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" id: "<PRODUCT_ID>") { id name descriptions { content } prices { list { amount } sale { amount } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response from response panel
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] product object returned with correct id
[DATA] descriptions[] non-empty; content populated
[MATH] prices.list.amount > 0
[DATA] prices.sale present (may be null if no sale active)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute query → same product returned
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; product null; descriptions empty; prices missing list amount

Title: xCatalog - Category Tree Query
Preconditions: Categories configured in store; GraphiQL UI loaded
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { categories(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount items { id name slug childCategories { id name } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response from response panel
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] Root categories returned; totalCount > 0
[DATA] childCategories nested correctly under parent
[DATA] Each category has slug (non-empty string suitable for URL)
[MATH] totalCount matches actual items[] length
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute query → same category tree
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; totalCount = 0; slug null or empty; childCategories missing on known parent categories

Title: xCatalog - Product Facets/Filters Query
Preconditions: Products with properties configured; GraphiQL UI loaded
Test_Data: storeId={{STORE_ID}}; facet includes brand/price range; filter combinations
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" facet: "brand") { totalCount items { id name } term_facets { name label terms { term label count isSelected } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — note facet values
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type filtered query with term facet applied
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — compare filtered vs unfiltered results
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] term_facets[] non-empty; at minimum brand facet present
[DATA] Each facet value has label and count > 0
[DATA] Filtered result set is smaller than unfiltered set
[DATA] Combining two filters uses AND logic (result set narrows further)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Filter counts match product count in filtered response
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; term_facets[] empty when facet-enabled properties exist; filter does not narrow results; facet count mismatches

Title: xCatalog - Product Variations Query
Preconditions: Configurable products with variations (size, color) exist in catalog; GraphiQL UI loaded
Test_Data: storeId={{STORE_ID}}; parentProductId (configurable product with known variations)
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { product(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" id: "<PARENT_ID>") { id name variations { id name properties { name value } prices { list { amount } } availabilityData { availableQuantity } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — verify variation properties and pricing
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data without error banner
[DATA] variations[] non-empty
[DATA] Each variation has distinct property combination (e.g., size+color unique)
[DATA] Each variation has prices.list.amount > 0
[DATA] availabilityData differs per variation if stock differs (or all show available if not tracked)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute query → same variations returned
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty in response panel; variations[] empty for known configurable product; all variations same price when they should differ; availabilityData not populated

Title: xCatalog - Product Listing Price During Reindex Lag Window
Preconditions: Product with known price; Elasticsearch indexing enabled; Admin API accessible; GraphiQL UI loaded
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type products query for {{TEST_SKU}} → Execute → Read response → record as originalPrice
[ACT] Change {{TEST_SKU}} price via: PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/pricelists/<priceListId>/prices with newPrice
[ACT] Immediately clear editor → type same products query → Execute
[READ] Read response — document whether old or new price returned (lag acceptable per BL-CROSS-002)
[WAIT] Wait 60 seconds for reindex to complete
[ACT] Clear editor → type same products query → Execute
[READ] Read response — verify price now reflects newPrice
Assertions:
[DATA] Step 4 result: originalPrice MAY be returned (acceptable reindex lag of 30-60s)
[DATA] Step 7 result: prices.list.amount = newPrice (reindex must have completed)
[DATA] If step 7 still shows originalPrice after 90s total: flag as reindex failure
[DATA] Document actual observed lag duration
[ERRORS] No errors[] in any query
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Multiple re-executions confirm price converges to newPrice
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: xCatalog still shows originalPrice after 90 seconds total; errors[] on xCatalog query; REST price update returns non-200; price shows as 0 after change
Cleanup: restore original price via Admin API

Title: xCatalog - Range Filter Syntax: Inclusive vs Exclusive Boundaries
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; products with known prices exist; auth not required for product queries
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" filter: "price.usd:[50 TO 100]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_Inclusive
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with exclusive bounds: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" filter: "price.usd:(50 TO 100)" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_Exclusive
Assertions:
[DATA] Inclusive [50 TO 100]: all items have price.actual.amount >= 50 AND <= 100
[DATA] Exclusive (50 TO 100): all items have price.actual.amount > 50 AND < 100
[MATH] Count_Inclusive >= Count_Exclusive (inclusive includes boundary products)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
[DATA] Products at exactly $50.00 or $100.00 appear in inclusive but not exclusive result
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute inclusive query → same totalCount
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: Inclusive and exclusive returning identical counts; products at exact boundary appearing in exclusive result; errors[] in response; totalCount = 0 for valid price range

Title: xCatalog - Open-Ended Range Filter Syntax
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; products with varying prices exist
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with upper-bounded range: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" filter: "price.usd:(TO 100]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_UpTo100
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with lower-bounded range: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" filter: "price.usd:[100 TO)" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name price { actual { amount } } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — note totalCount as Count_From100
Assertions:
[DATA] (TO 100]: all items have price.actual.amount <= 100
[DATA] [100 TO): all items have price.actual.amount >= 100
[MATH] Count_UpTo100 + Count_From100 >= total products (products at exactly $100 counted in both)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in either response
[DATA] No products with amount > 100 in the (TO 100] result
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute each query → same totalCount
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: Products above $100 appearing in (TO 100] result; products below $100 in [100 TO) result; errors[] in response; empty result for ranges containing known products

Title: xCatalog - Range Filter on Non-Price Property (Underscore Field Name)
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; products with underscore-named property exist (e.g., Volume_ml in Alcoholic Drinks category)
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query filtering by underscore property: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" filter: "Volume_ml:[200 TO 550]" facet: "Volume_ml" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount items { name properties { name value } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read response — check totalCount and item properties
Assertions:
[DATA] totalCount > 0 for valid Volume_ml range (observed: 12 products)
[DATA] Returned items have Volume_ml property values within 200–550 range (observed: 200, 220, 330, 500, 550)
[DATA] No items with Volume_ml < 200 (e.g., 33ml products excluded)
[ERRORS] No errors[] related to filter logic (note: ARGUMENT_NULL errors may appear for items with null properties — this is a pre-existing data quality issue, not a filter bug)
[DATA] Filter parameter 'Volume_ml' is preserved in the request (not truncated to 'Volume')
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → consistent results
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: totalCount = 0 for range containing known products; items with Volume_ml=33 appearing in 200–550 range; field name truncated in filter parameter; errors[] indicating filter parsing failure

Title: xCatalog - Facet Parameter Returns Range Aggregation Bucket
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI loaded; category with price-range products exists
Steps:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query with explicit range facet parameter: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" facet: "price.usd:[0 TO 100]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount range_facets { name ranges { from to count isSelected label } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[WAIT] Response panel shows result
[READ] Read range_facets in response
[ACT] Clear editor → type query with different range bucket: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" facet: "price.usd:[100 TO 500]" currencyCode: "USD") { totalCount range_facets { name ranges { from to count isSelected label } } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read range_facets in response — compare bucket count
Assertions:
[DATA] range_facets array contains entry with name 'price'
[DATA] ranges[] contains at least one bucket object with from, to, count, isSelected, label fields
[MATH] Bucket count is non-negative and <= totalCount
[DATA] isSelected reflects whether the range matches the facet parameter
[ERRORS] No errors[] in response
[DATA] Second query returns a different bucket count for [100 TO 500] range
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute → same bucket counts
[CONSOLE] No JS errors in GraphiQL
Failure_Signals: range_facets empty or null (note: facet:"price" alone returns empty range_facets — explicit range syntax required); bucket missing count field; errors[] in response; bucket count exceeds totalCount
